Given,

 tan-1cos15π4-1sinπ4

=-π8

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo></math>. be consecutive natural numbers. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mn>2021</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mn>2022</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>4</mn></msub><mo>,</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo></math>. be consecutive natural numbers. Then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>1</mn></msub><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msub><msub><mi>a</mi><mn>3</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>+</mo><mo>…</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>.</mo><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mn>2021</mn></msub><msub><mi mathvariant="normal">a</mi><mn>2022</mn></msub></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

tan-111+a1a2+tan-111+a2a3................tan-111+a2021a2022

=tan-1a2-a11+a1a2+tan-1a3-a21+a2a3................tan-1a2022-a20211+a2021a2022

=tan-1a2-tan-1a1+tan-1a3-tan-1a2..........+tan-1a2022-tan-1a2021

using the formula  tan-1x-tan-1y=tan-1x-y1+xy

=tan-1a2022-tan-1a1

=tan-1a2022-tan-11 as given a1=1

=tan-1a2022-π4

=π2-cot-1a2022-π4

=π4-cot-1a2022

=π4-cot-12022

As a1=1, a2=2 .... and so on a2022=2022

q: Considering the principal values of the inverse trigonometric functions, the sum of all the solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: Considering the principal values of the inverse trigonometric functions, the sum of all the solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>-</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mi>x</mi></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given,

cos-1x-2sin-1x=cos-12x

⇒cos-1x-2π2-cos-1x=cos-12x

⇒cos-1x-π+2cos-1x=cos-12x

⇒3cos-1x=π+cos-12x     ...1

Taking cos both side we get,

⇒cos3cos-1x=cosπ+cos-12x

⇒coscos-14x3-3x=-coscos-12x

⇒4x3-3x=-2x

⇒4x3=x

⇒x=0,±12

All will satisfy the original equation,

So, sum of all the solution will be 0+12-12=0

solution:
Given,

tan2tan-115+sec-152+2tan-118

=tan2tan-115+tan-118+tan-112

=tan2tan-115+181-140+tan-112

=tan2tan-113+tan-112

=tantan-12×131-132+tan-112

=tantan-134+tan-112

=tantan-134+121-38

=tantan-15458

=tantan-12

=2

q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>⊂</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> be the largest interval for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, holds . If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to;
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>a</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>b</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>⊂</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math> be the largest interval for which <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mi>θ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>></mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mi>θ</mi><mo>∈</mo><mfenced><mrow><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow></mfenced></math>, holds . If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>6</mn><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>10</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>0</mn></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>b</mi><mo>-</mo><mi>a</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi></math> is equal to;
solution:
Given, sin-1sinθ-cos-1sinθ>0,θ∈0,2π

∵sin-1θ+cos-1θ=π2

∴sin-1sinθ-π2-sin-1sinθ>0

sin-1sinθ>π4⇒θ∈π4,3π4

a,b=π4,3π4⇒b-a=π2

Given b-a=α-β

∴ α-β=π2  .....(1)

Now αx2+βx+sin-1x2-6x+10+cos-1x2-6x+10=0

Now defining x2-6x+10=1+x-32≥1

Hence x=3 is the only possible solution

9α+3β+π2=0       …2

On solving equations 1 and 2 we get,

α=π12

subject: Mathematics
q: If the domain of the function $f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x-5}{11-3 x}\right)+\sin ^{-1}\left(2 x^{2}-3 x+1\right)$ is the interval $[\alpha, \beta]$, then $\alpha+2 \beta$ is equal to :
question: If the domain of the function $f(x)=\cos ^{-1}\left(\frac{2 x-5}{11-3 x}\right)+\sin ^{-1}\left(2 x^{2}-3 x+1\right)$ is the interval $[\alpha, \beta]$, then $\alpha+2 \beta$ is equal to :
solution:
For $\cos^{-1}\left(\frac{2x-5}{11-3x}\right)$: $-1 \leq \frac{2x-5}{11-3x} \leq 1$ gives $x \leq \frac{16}{5}$ or $x > 6$.
For $\sin^{-1}(2x^2 - 3x + 1)$: $-1 \leq 2x^2 - 3x + 1 \leq 1$.
Lower bound always satisfied (discriminant $< 0$).
Upper bound gives $x(2x-3) \leq 0$, so $0 \leq x \leq \frac{3}{2}$.
Intersection of both conditions: $[0, \frac{3}{2}]$.
Thus $\alpha = 0$, $\beta = \frac{3}{2}$.
$\alpha + 2\beta = 0 + 3 = 3$.

subject: Mathematics
q: The integral $\int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \cos \left(2 \cot ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}}\right) d x$ is equal to
question: The integral $\int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \cos \left(2 \cot ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}}\right) d x$ is equal to
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & I=\int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \cos \left(2 \cot ^{-1}\left(\sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}}\right) d x\right) \\ & \int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \cos \left(2\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1+x}}\right)\right) d x \\ & \left.\int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \frac{1-\tan ^2\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}}\right)}{1+\tan ^2\left(\tan ^{-1} \sqrt{\frac{1+x}{1-x}}\right.}\right) d x\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & =\int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \frac{1-\left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right)}{1+\left(\frac{1+x}{1-x}\right)} d x=\int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \frac{-2 x}{2} d x \\ & =\int_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4}(-x) d x=-\left(\frac{x^2}{2}\right)_{1 / 4}^{3 / 4} \\ & =-\frac{1}{2}\left[\frac{9}{16}-\frac{1}{16}\right] \\ & =-\frac{1}{4}\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>x</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mtext>and</mtext><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>)</mo></math> denotes the number of elements in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> then :
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mi>R</mi><mo>:</mo><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>x</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo> </mo><mtext>and</mtext><mo> </mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>-</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow><mrow><mn>1</mn><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>n</mi><mo>(</mo><mi>S</mi><mo>)</mo></math> denotes the number of elements in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> then :
options: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn></math> and only one element in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> is less then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>

<math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi mathvariant="normal">n</mi><mo>(</mo><mi mathvariant="normal">S</mi><mo>)</mo><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></math> and the element in <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>S</mi></math> is more than <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>2</mn></mfrac></math>
solution:
Given, 0<x<1

2tan-11-x1+x=cos-11-x21+x2

Put x=tanθ    ; 0<θ<π4

2tan-11-tan θ1+tan θ=cos-11-tan2θ1+tan2θ

⇒2tan-1tanπ4-θ=cos-1(cos2θ)

2π4-θ=2θ⇒θ=π8

Put θ=tan-1x we get,

x=tanπ8∴x=2-1≃0.414<12

subject: Mathematics
q: If $\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{5}\right)+\operatorname{cosec}^{-1}\left(\frac{5}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}$ then a value of $x$ is
question: If $\sin ^{-1}\left(\frac{x}{5}\right)+\operatorname{cosec}^{-1}\left(\frac{5}{4}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}$ then a value of $x$ is
solution:
$\sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{5}+\sin ^{-1} \frac{4}{5}=\frac{\pi}{2}$
$\Rightarrow \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{5}=\cos ^{-1} \frac{4}{5} \Rightarrow \sin ^{-1} \frac{x}{5}=\sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{5}$
$\therefore x=3$.

subject: Mathematics
q: For <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>π</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>−</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>γ</mi></math> equal to
question: For <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>α</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>,</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn></math>. If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>γ</mi><mo>=</mo><mi>π</mi></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>γ</mi></mrow></mfenced><mfenced><mrow><mi>α</mi><mo>−</mo><mi>γ</mi><mo>+</mo><mi>β</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mn>3</mn><mi>α</mi><mi>β</mi></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>γ</mi></math> equal to
solution:
Given: sin−1α+sin−1β+sin−1γ=π

Let, sin−1α=A, sin−1β=B, sin−1γ=C

⇒A+B+C=π

Also, α=sinA, β=sinB, γ=sinC   ...i

It is given that, α+β2−γ2=3αβ.

⇒α2+β2−γ2=αβ

⇒sin2A+sin2B-sin2C=sinAsinB

⇒sin2A+sinB+CsinB-C=sinAsinB

⇒sin2A+sinπ-AsinB-C=sinAsinB

⇒sin2A+sinAsinB-C=sinAsinB

⇒sinAsinA+sinB-C=sinAsinB

⇒sinAsinA+sinB-C-sinAsinB=0

⇒sinAsinA+sinB-C-sinB=0

⇒sinAsinB+C+sinB-C-sinB=0

⇒sinA2sinBcosC-sinB=0

⇒sinAsinB2cosC-1=0

⇒sinA=0 or sinB=0 or cosC=12

It is given that, α,β,γ≠0

⇒cosC=12

⇒sinC=32

⇒γ=32

subject: Mathematics
q: For <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mo>(</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>]</mo></math>, the number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi></math> is equal to
question: For <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mo>(</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>]</mo></math>, the number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi></math> is equal to
solution:
Given that sin-1x=2tan-1x

⇒sin-1x=sin-12x1+x2

⇒x=2x1+x2

⇒x+x3=2x

⇒x3-x=0

⇒x(x2-1)=0

⇒x=0,-1,1.

But x∈(-1,1], so the number of roots are 2, x=0 & 1

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mn>5</mn></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mn>5</mn></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
question: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>a</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mn>5</mn></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>b</mi><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>−</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mn>5</mn></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math>, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>a</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>b</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> is equal to
solution:
Given: a=sin-1sin5 and b=cos-1cos5

⇒a=5-2π and b=2π-5

⇒a2+b2=5-2π2+2π-52

⇒a2+b2=25+4π2-20π+4π2+25-20π

⇒a2+b2=8π2-40π+50

subject: Mathematics
q: $\cos \left(\sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{5}+\sin ^{-1} \frac{5}{13}+\sin ^{-1} \frac{33}{65}\right)$ is equal to:
question: $\cos \left(\sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{5}+\sin ^{-1} \frac{5}{13}+\sin ^{-1} \frac{33}{65}\right)$ is equal to:
solution: $\begin{aligned} & \cos \left(\sin ^{-1} \frac{3}{5}+\sin ^{-1} \frac{5}{13}+\sin ^{-1} \frac{33}{65}\right) \\ & \cos \left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{3}{4}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{5}{12}+\tan ^{-1} \frac{33}{56}\right) \\ & \cos \left(\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{\frac{3}{4}+\frac{5}{12}}{1+\frac{3}{4} \cdot \frac{5}{12}}\right)+\tan ^{-1} \frac{33}{56}\right) \\ & \cos \left(\tan ^{-1} \frac{56}{33}+\cot ^{-1} \frac{56}{33}\right) \\ & \cos \left(\frac{\pi}{2}\right)=0\end{aligned}$

subject: Mathematics
q: $\cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{\cos \alpha})=\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\cos \alpha})=x$, then $\sin x=$
question: $\cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{\cos \alpha})=\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\cos \alpha})=x$, then $\sin x=$
solution:
$\cot ^{-1}(\sqrt{\cos \alpha})-\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\cos \alpha})=x$
$\tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{\cos \alpha}}\right)-\tan ^{-1}(\sqrt{\cos \alpha})=x \Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} \frac{\frac{1}{\sqrt{\cos \alpha}}-\sqrt{\cos \alpha}}{1+\frac{1}{\sqrt{\cos \alpha}} \cdot \sqrt{\cos \alpha}}=x$
$\Rightarrow \tan ^{-1} \frac{1-\cos \alpha}{2 \sqrt{\cos \alpha}}=x \Rightarrow \tan x=\frac{1-\cos \alpha}{2 \sqrt{\cos \alpha}}$
OR $\cot x=\frac{2 \sqrt{\cos \alpha}}{1-\cos \alpha}$ or $\operatorname{cosec} x=\frac{1+\cos \alpha}{1-\cos \alpha}$
$\sin x=\frac{1-\cos \alpha}{1+\cos \alpha}=\frac{1-\left(1-2 \sin ^2 \alpha / 2\right)}{1+2 \cos ^2 \alpha / 2-1}$ or $\sin x=\tan ^2 \frac{\alpha}{2}$

subject: Mathematics
q: Considering only the principal values of inverse functions, the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>
question: Considering only the principal values of inverse functions, the set <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>A</mi><mo>=</mo><mfenced open="{" close="}" separators="|"><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>≥</mo><mn>0</mn><mo> </mo><mo>:</mo><mo> </mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced separators="|"><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math>
solution:
Given tan-12x+tan-13x=π4

⇒tan-12x+3x1-2x×3x=π4

Applying tangent on both sides,

⇒5x1-6x2=1 ∵tanπ4=1

⇒5x=1-6x2

⇒6x2+5x-1=0

⇒6x2+6x-x-1=0

⇒6x-1x+1=0

⇒x=-1, 16

But, since x≥0.

Hence, x=16

∴ A has only one element and thus, it is a singleton.

subject: Mathematics
q: The sum of possible values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cot</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>8</mn><mn>31</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is:
question: The sum of possible values of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cot</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>8</mn><mn>31</mn></mfrac></mfenced></math> is:
solution:
Given tan-1x+1+cot-11x-1=tan-1831

⇒tan-1x+1+tan-1x-1=tan-1831,   ∴tan-1x=cot-11x, x>0

⇒tan-1x+1+x-11-x+1x-1=tan-1831

⇒tan-12x1-x+1x-1=tan-1831

Taking tangent both the sides we get, 

(x+1)+(x-1)1-x2-1=831

⇒2x2-x2=831

⇒4x2+31x-8=0

⇒x=-8,14

But, if x=14

tan-1x+1∈0,π2

and cot-11x-1∈π2,π

⇒LHS>π2 and RHS<π2 (Not possible)

Hence, x=-8=-324.

subject: Mathematics
q: Given that the inverse trigonometric functions take principal values only. Then, the number of real values of x which satisfy sin-13x5+sin-14x5=sin-1x is equal to:
question: Given that the inverse trigonometric functions take principal values only. Then, the number of real values of x which satisfy sin-13x5+sin-14x5=sin-1x is equal to:
solution:
sin-13x5+sin-14x5=sin-1x

sin-13x51-16x225+4x51-9x225=sin-1x

3x51-16x225+4x51-9x225=x

x=0, 325-16x2+425-9x2=25

425-9x2=25-325-16x2 

squaring we get

1625-9x2=625+925-16x2-15025-16x2

400=625+225-15025-16x2

25-16x2=3⇒25-16x2=9

⇒x2=1

Put x=0,1,-1 in the original equation We see that all values satisfy the original equation.

Number of solution =3

subject: Mathematics
q: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>S</mi><mi>k</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi>k</mi></munderover><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><msup><mn>6</mn><mi>r</mi></msup><mrow><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>3</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi>lim</mi><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>→</mo><mo>∞</mo></mrow></munder><msub><mi>S</mi><mi>k</mi></msub></math> is equal to :
question: Let <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msub><mi>S</mi><mi>k</mi></msub><mo>=</mo><munderover><mo>∑</mo><mrow><mi>r</mi><mo>=</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow><mi>k</mi></munderover><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><msup><mn>6</mn><mi>r</mi></msup><mrow><msup><mn>2</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mo>+</mo><msup><mn>3</mn><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>r</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup></mrow></mfrac></mfenced><mo>,</mo></math> then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><munder><mi>lim</mi><mrow><mi>k</mi><mo>→</mo><mo>∞</mo></mrow></munder><msub><mi>S</mi><mi>k</mi></msub></math> is equal to :
solution:
Given Sk=∑r=1ktan-16r22r+1+32r+1

⇒Sk=∑r=1ktan-12r·3r22r·2+32r·3

Divide by 32r we get, 

Sk=∑r=1ktan-123r232r.2+3

Sk=∑r=1ktan-123r3232r+1+1

Let 23r=t

Sk=∑r=1ktan-1t31+23t2

Sk=∑r=1ktan-1t-2t31+t.2t3

Sk=∑r=1ktan-1t-tan-12t3

Sk=∑r=1ktan-123r-tan-123r+1

Sk=tan-123-tan-1232+tan-1232-tan-1233+...+tan-123k-tan-123k+1

Sk=tan-123-tan-123k+1

Then, S∞=limk→∞tan-123-tan-123k+1

=tan-123-tan-10

∴ S∞=tan-123=cot-132,  ∵tan-1x=cot-11x

q: If <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><msup><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mi>x</mi></mrow></mfenced><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>=</mo><mi>a</mi><mo>;</mo><mo> </mo><mn>0</mn><mo><</mo><mi>x</mi><mo><</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mo> </mo><mi>a</mi><mo>≠</mo><mn>0</mn><mo>,</mo></math> then the value of <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mn>2</mn><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></math> is

solution:
sin-1x2-cos-1x2=a

Let sin-1x=t

x=sin t

sin-1sin t2-cos-1sin t2=a

t2-π2-t2=a

t2-π24+t2-πt=a

πt-π24=a

t=aπ+π4

x=sinaπ+π4

2x2-1=2sin2aπ+π4-1

=2sinaπ cosπ4+cosaπ sinπ42-1

=sinaπ+cosaπ2-1

=sin2aπ+cos2aπ+2cosaπsinaπ-1

=2cosaπsinaπ

=sin2aπ

subject: Mathematics
q: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>6</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
question: <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>sin</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>2</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>cos</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>7</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>6</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mi>tan</mi><mfrac><mrow><mn>3</mn><mi>π</mi></mrow><mn>4</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Given, sin-1sin2π3+cos-1cos7π6+tan-1tan3π4

Now simplifying each term we get, 

sin-1sin2π3=π-2π3=π3

cos-1cos7π6=2π-7π6=5π6

tan-1tan3π4=3π4-π=-π4

sin-1sin2π3+cos-1cos7π6+tan-1tan3π4

=π3+5π6-π4

=11π12

subject: Mathematics
q:
cos-1(cos(-5))+sin-1(sin(6))-tan-1(tan(12)) is equal to :
(The inverse trigonometric functions take the principal values)
question:
cos-1(cos(-5))+sin-1(sin(6))-tan-1(tan(12)) is equal to :
(The inverse trigonometric functions take the principal values)
solution:
cos-1(cos(-5))+sin-1sin(6)-tan-1(tan(12))

=2π-5+(-2π+6)-(12-4π)

=4π-11

q: The number of real roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> is:
question: The number of real roots of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msqrt><mi>x</mi><mfenced><mrow><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></mfenced></msqrt><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><msqrt><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>+</mo><mn>1</mn></msqrt><mo>=</mo><mfrac><mi>π</mi><mn>4</mn></mfrac></math> is:
solution:
We have,

tan-1x2+x+sin-1x2+x+1=π4

For equation to be defined,

for tan-1xx+1, 

x2+x≥0

⇒x2+x+1≥1  ....1

And, from domain of sin-1x2+x+1, x2+x+1≤1   ....2

∴ from 1 & 2 only possibility that the equation is defined is

x2+x=0 

⇒x=0, -1

at x=0, tan-1xx+1+sin-1x2+x+1=0+π2≠π4

and at x=-1, tan-1xx+1+sin-1x2+x+1=0+π2≠π4

None of these values satisfy

∴ Number of roots=0

Then, the minimum value of $x^2+y^2+2 x y \sin \alpha$ is
question:
Given that the inverse trigonometric function assumes principal values only. Let $x$, $y$ be any two real numbers in $[-1,1]$ such that $\cos ^{-1} x-\sin ^{-1} y=\alpha, \frac{-\pi}{2} \leq \alpha \leq \pi$.
Then, the minimum value of $x^2+y^2+2 x y \sin \alpha$ is
solution:
$\begin{aligned} & \cos ^{-1} x-\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\cos ^{-1} y\right)=\alpha \\ & \cos ^{-1} x+\cos ^{-1} y=\frac{\pi}{2}+\alpha \\ & \alpha \in\left[-\frac{\pi}{2}, \pi\right], \frac{\pi}{2}+\alpha \in\left[0, \frac{3 \pi}{2}\right] \\ & \cos ^{-1}\left(x y-\sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-y^2}\right)=\frac{\pi}{2}+\alpha \\ & x y-\sqrt{1-x^2} \sqrt{1-y^2}=-\sin \alpha \\ & (x y+\sin \alpha)=\left(1-x^2\right)\left(1-y^2\right) \\ & x^2 y^2+2 x y \sin a+\sin ^2 a=1-x^2-y^2+x^2 y^2 \\ & x^2+y^2+2 x y \sin \alpha=1-\sin ^2 \alpha \\ & x^2+y^2+2 x y \sin \alpha=\cos ^2 \alpha \end{aligned}$
Min. value of $\cos ^2 \alpha=0$ At $\alpha=\frac{\pi}{2}$

q: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mo>[</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>]</mo></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></math> denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is :<gwmw style="display:none;"></gwmw>
question: The number of solutions of the equation <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>sin</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>1</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced close="]" open="["><mrow><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup><mo>-</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mrow></mfenced><mo>=</mo><msup><mi>x</mi><mn>2</mn></msup></math> for <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>∈</mo><mo>[</mo><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>,</mo><mn>1</mn><mo>]</mo></math>, and <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mo>[</mo><mi>x</mi><mo>]</mo></math> denotes the greatest integer less than or equal to <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><mi>x</mi><mo>,</mo></math> is :<gwmw style="display:none;"></gwmw>
solution:
Given equation is

sin-1x2+13+cos-1x2-23=x2

Now, sin-1x2+13 is defined if -1≤x2+13≤1

⇒-1≤x2+13<2

⇒-43≤x2<53

⇒0≤x2<53   ...1

Also, and cos-1x2-23 is defined if -1≤x2-23≤1

 ⇒-1≤x2-23<2

⇒-13≤x2<83

⇒0≤x2<83   ...2

So, from (1) and (2) we can conclude 0≤x2<53

Case -I: If 0≤x2<23

⇒sin-1(0)+cos-1(-1)=x2

⇒0+π=x2

⇒x2=π but π∉0,23

⇒ No value of 'x'

Case - II: If 23≤x2<53

⇒sin-1(1)+cos-1(0)=x2

⇒π2+π2=x2

⇒x2=π but π∉23,53

⇒ No value of 'x'

So, number of solutions of the equation is zero.

question: If the inverse trigonometric functions take principal values, then <math xmlns="http://www.w3.org/1998/Math/MathML"><msup><mi>cos</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mrow><mfrac><mn>3</mn><mn>10</mn></mfrac><mi>cos</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mfenced></mrow></mfenced><mo>+</mo><mfrac><mn>2</mn><mn>5</mn></mfrac><mi>sin</mi><mfenced><mrow><msup><mi>tan</mi><mrow><mo>-</mo><mn>1</mn></mrow></msup><mfenced><mfrac><mn>4</mn><mn>3</mn></mfrac></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></mrow></mfenced></math> is equal to
solution:
Let tan-143=θ⇒tanθ=43

∴cosθ=35 & sinθ=45

So, cos-1310costan-143+25sintan-143

=cos-1310cosθ+25sinθ

=cos-1310×35+25×45

=cos-1950+825

=cos-112=π3
